title: SQL Injection (SQLi)
description: Untrusted user input is directly concatenated or interpolated into a SQL command string instead of using parameterized queries, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary SQL statements.
example_flaw:
query = f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = '{user_input}'"
cursor.execute(query)
example_fix:
query = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = %s"
cursor.execute(query, (user_input,))

title: OS Command Injection
description: User input is passed directly to system shells or commands without proper sanitization or without using safe API execution wrappers, permitting execution of arbitrary OS commands.

title: Hardcoded Credentials or Secrets
description: Sensitive credentials, passwords, API tokens, encryption keys, or secrets are hardcoded in the codebase instead of being stored in environment variables or configuration files.

title: Path Traversal (Directory Traversal)
description: Accepting file paths from users without checking if they contain traversal sequences (like '../') or ensuring they remain inside the intended directory boundary.
example_flaw:
with open(os.path.join("uploads", filename), "r") as f:
    data = f.read()
example_fix:
safe_path = os.path.abspath(os.path.join("uploads", filename))
if not safe_path.startswith(os.path.abspath("uploads")):
    raise PermissionError("Access denied")
with open(safe_path, "r") as f:
    data = f.read()

title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
description: User input is rendered on a webpage without escaping or sanitization, allowing attackers to inject malicious scripts that execute in the victim's browser context.
example_fix:
from html import escape return HTMLResponse(f"
Hello {escape(name)}
")

title: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)
description: State-changing operations are allowed without verifying an anti-CSRF token or validation header, permitting third-party sites to perform unauthorized actions on behalf of the user.
example_fix:
app.add_middleware(SessionMiddleware, secret_key="secret")
# Enable CSRF middleware or set SameSite=Strict cookies

title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
description: The application makes arbitrary outgoing HTTP/network requests to a URL supplied by the user, exposing internal systems, metadata endpoints, or local networks.

title: Insecure CORS Policy
description: The application configures Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) with a wildcard origin ('*') combined with credential support or allows untrusted origins, exposing sensitive data to other websites.
